title: tasks-mux: ExternalTrackerBackend -- sync tasks to Jira/Linear/REST
userRequest: Create implementation plan for issue #632: tasks-mux: ExternalTrackerBackend -- sync tasks to Jira/Linear/REST. Note: this depends on foundational work that is being implemented in parallel.
issueBody: New backend that syncs tasks to external issue trackers as a responder type. Deliverables: ExternalTrackerBackend implementing BreakpointBackend; pluggable tracker adapters: GitHubIssues (existing), Jira (new), Linear (new), GenericREST (new); bidirectional sync: task state changes flow both ways; webhook listener for push notifications from trackers; field mapping configuration. Depends on #620 in original body, but latest comment updates dependency to #630. Related/supersedes #583. Design: docs/agent-mux-babysitter-integrations/tasks-mux-routing.md

issueComments.author: tmuskal
issueComments.body: Depends on: #630 (ResponderType system). Supersedes: #583 (tasks-mux-native version of external trackers).

issueComments.author: a5c-ai
issueComments.body: Triage says the gap is tracker responder semantics: outbound sync, inbound webhook/status sync, provider-specific field mapping, conflict handling, idempotency, and auth boundaries. Recommended staged approach: define tracker responder/backend contract and config model; add provider-neutral mapping and sync primitives; refactor or wrap GitHubIssuesBackend behind tracker abstraction while preserving behavior; add Jira, Linear, and GenericREST adapters; add webhook ingestion; wire tracker routing after #630 lands; add SDK/agent-platform integration only after tasks-mux backend contracts are covered by tests.

latestDependencyNote: Use #630, not the stale #620 reference in the issue body, as the active dependency for the ResponderType system.

dependencyContext.issueTitle: tasks-mux: add ResponderType system (human, agent, tracker, internal, auto)
dependencyContext.summary: Foundational typed responder routing must define ResponderType, responder metadata, and TaskRouter. Issue #632 should consume that contract and avoid duplicating it.
expectedContract: ResponderType includes human, agent, tracker, internal, and auto or an equivalent routing mode.
Tracker responders can carry trackerBackend/trackerConfig metadata.
TaskRouter can match tracker responders by type, capabilities, availability, and hints.
Existing human responder and BreakpointBackend behavior remains compatible.

reuseAuditFindings.processLibraryStatus: .a5c/process-library/ was requested but is not present in this checkout; use docs/agent-reference/process-authoring.md and existing .a5c/processes conventions instead.
matchedExistingInfrastructure: packages/tasks-mux/src/backend.ts defines the BreakpointBackend lifecycle contract to implement.
packages/tasks-mux/src/types.ts defines BackendConfigSchema for git-native, server, and github-issues only.
packages/tasks-mux/src/backends/index.ts has a backend factory registry with git-native, github-issues, and server.
packages/tasks-mux/src/backends/github-issues.ts already maps breakpoints to GitHub issues with hidden payload markers and answer parsing.
packages/tasks-mux/routing.json shows existing github-issues routing config examples.
packages/tasks-mux/src/auth/types.ts includes webhookSecret patterns for GitHub app auth.
packages/agent-platform/src/daemon/webhookListener.ts has a simple HTTP webhook listener pattern, but package boundaries must be evaluated before reuse.
absentInfrastructure: No Jira, Linear, or Generic REST tracker adapters exist.
No provider-neutral tracker sync cursor, event id, or conflict-resolution model exists in tasks-mux.
No tasks-mux-local webhook ingestion surface exists for tracker updates.
No field mapping configuration schema exists for tracker-backed tasks.
No bulk sync implementation is requested in #632 despite being listed as a broader gap.
implementationConstraints: Do not implement #630 ResponderType/TaskRouter in this issue.
Do not add live-provider tests that require Jira, Linear, GitHub, or arbitrary REST credentials.
Preserve GitHubIssuesBackend behavior and durable gh-{number} ids.
Keep credentials and webhook secrets out of serialized breakpoint content, logs, and snapshots.
